title: Gröna börsbolag hiss eller diss?
summary_md:
# Gröna börsbolag hiss eller diss?

**Feed**: placerapodden  
**Published**: 2026-06-18  
**Market Sentiment**: Negative

⏱️ **Time Horizon Key**: ST = <3 months | MT = 3-12 months | LT = >12 months

---

## Episode Thesis

Columnist and academic Christian Sandström returns to review a year of green-transition investments, remaining deeply skeptical. He frames the Stegra (now Green Nexus) financing debacle, Sinis Fertilizer's bankruptcy, and Powercell's overstated fuel-cell claims as symptoms of overoptimism, subsidy-dependence, and startups attempting mega-projects beyond their competence. He argues wind-power projectors face structural losses from cannibalisation, dismisses small modular nuclear hype, and instead favours 'quiet champions' like Fagerhult that deliver incremental green development without spades-in-the-ground fanfare.

---

## Universe Stocks

### ⛔ PCELL — Powercell | **AVOID** (Strong)

**Sentiment**: Negative | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 80%

SVT/NRK exposed ~10x overstatement of fuel-cell durability; persistent grant dependence and rejected/late reporting to Energimyndigheten.

#### Reasoning

Sandström views grant-seeking firms that systematise subsidies over 15-year horizons as fundamentally uncompetitive ('bidragskapitalister'). Hydrogen/fuel-cell tech is perennially 'future technology' that never works in practice; the verified tenfold overstatement of operating hours and continued public funding despite exposés are red flags, not positives.

#### Sell Triggers

- if independent verification confirms durability claims at scale
- if firm reaches profitability without grant funding
- if hydrogen demand becomes commercially viable

> *"Det var snarare kanske att man låg runt 3000 timmar. Så det är alltså en överdrift med en faktor 10 på vad tekniken faktiskt kan göra."*

---

### ⛔ EOLU-B — Eolus Vind | **AVOID** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Negative | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 60%

Last listed wind projector; stock down 1-2 years; sector hit by overestablishment, minus prices, rising losses and cannibalisation.

#### Reasoning

Affärsvärlden has long documented severe profitability problems in Swedish wind: growing minus-prices, rising exports, and cannibalisation where more turbines depress prices and deepen losses (operating margins ~-45-50% in 2024, likely worse 2025). Asset values for the plants projectors build will fall as profitability erodes, eventually hitting developers.

#### Sell Triggers

- if power prices and capture rates recover
- if wind overcapacity is absorbed
- if grid/storage build-out lifts realised prices

> *"ju mer vindkraft du bygger desto sämre blir lönsamheten för vindkraft därför att det genereras ännu större överskott när det blåser och då blir priserna ännu lägre"*

---

### ⛔ ORRON — Orrön Energy | **AVOID** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Negative | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 50%

Holds wind-power exposure amid deteriorating sector economics, plus an unresolved dispute over an alleged breach of international law.

#### Reasoning

Listed among wind-exposed actors that should be treated with caution given the structural loss-making in Swedish wind; additionally encumbered by a legal/international-law dispute that adds idiosyncratic risk on top of the sector headwinds. Sandström views the sector but leaves the buy/sell call to the listener.

#### Sell Triggers

- if the legal dispute resolves favourably
- if wind-power economics improve
- if non-wind assets prove resilient

> *"Sen har vi ju Orrön Energy då, som ju har en del vindkraft. Men det är ju en del annat som ligger runt orrön med något tvist om ett folkrättsbrott."*

---

### 🟠 FAGR — Fagerhult | **WATCH** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 60%

Cited as a 'quiet champion' of incremental green development via LED and smart lighting that economises electricity use without subsidies or fanfare.

#### Reasoning

Sandström reframes 'green transition' as 'green development' — small cumulative steps of tech, legislation and innovation that improve resource efficiency. Swedish engineering firms and Fagerhult's shift to LED and smart lighting exemplify quietly profitable green progress, which he says investors should look at instead of hyped revolution plays.

#### Sell Triggers

- if LED/smart-lighting margins compress
- if competitive position erodes
- if the efficiency thesis fails to translate to returns

> *"Där är ju svensk verkstadsindustri men även bolag som exempelvis Fagerhult med övergången till både LED-lampor och sen nu mer av det man kallar för smart lightning"*

---

### 🟡 PEAB-B — Peab | **HOLD** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Neutral | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 50%

Has earned money building wind farms but is diversified across many business areas, so not directly exposed to wind-sector deterioration.

#### Reasoning

Unlike pure-play projectors and turbine makers, contractors like Peab earned money pouring concrete and building wind plants but have many other segments, insulating them from the wind-economics downturn. Presented as a relatively safe construction exposure rather than a green-thesis bet.

#### Sell Triggers

- if construction demand broadly weakens
- if wind-related revenue was larger than assumed
- if margins deteriorate across segments

> *"Ett bolag som Peab har ju tjänat pengar på vindkraft på att vara med och bygga och kanske gjuta betong och så. Men de har ju många andra affärsområden."*

---

## Summary Table

| Ticker | Company | Signal | TH | Conf | Context |
|--------|---------|--------|----|------|---------|
| **PCELL** | Powercell | ⛔ AVOID | LT | 80% | SVT/NRK exposed ~10x overstatement of fuel-cell durability; persistent grant dependence and rejected/late reporting to Energimyndigheten. |
| **EOLU-B** | Eolus Vind | ⛔ AVOID | LT | 60% | Last listed wind projector; stock down 1-2 years; sector hit by overestablishment, minus prices, rising losses and cannibalisation. |
| **ORRON** | Orrön Energy | ⛔ AVOID | MT | 50% | Holds wind-power exposure amid deteriorating sector economics, plus an unresolved dispute over an alleged breach of international law. |
| **FAGR** | Fagerhult | 🟠 WATCH | LT | 60% | Cited as a 'quiet champion' of incremental green development via LED and smart lighting that economises electricity use without subsidies or fanfare. |
| **PEAB-B** | Peab | 🟡 HOLD | LT | 50% | Has earned money building wind farms but is diversified across many business areas, so not directly exposed to wind-sector deterioration. |
